steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The data shows mammal studies had a larger average effect than amphibian studies overall, yet some individual amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average. The correct answer must follow logically from that overlap.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — some studies of amphibians found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of mammals did.: if some amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average, they necessarily exceeded at least some individual mammal studies — this follows directly from the data. ✓
  • Choice B — the studies in the meta-analysis that examined mammals were more likely than those that examined amphibians to specify whether the observed effects were detrimental.: makes a claim about reporting practices that the data does not address. ✗
  • Choice C — the differences that studies attribute to exposure to anthropogenic noise are likely to be more pronounced for amphibians than they are for mammals.: reverses the stated averages — the passage says mammals had the larger average, not amphibians. ✗
  • Choice D — the difference found in the study conducted by Debra M. Shier and colleagues was likely larger than the average difference for studies of Stephens kangaroo rats included in the meta-analysis.: makes a specific claim about Shier's study that cannot be verified from the graph alone. ✗
That matches Choice A — some studies of amphibians found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of mammals did..
